title: Passaging Cancer Organoid Cultures
author: Hazel Rogers
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfe3jjgn

steps.instruction: Using a P1000 pipette, detach BME2 drops from the plate. (Optional - detach BME2 drops using a cell-scraper; recommended for passaging a large number of plates).
Pipette suspension up and down multiple times to dissociate organoids from BME2 and transfer to an approproate size tube using a P1000 pipette or 10 mL stripette.
Wash wells with TrypLE (approx 1 mL per well of a 6 well plate) to collect any remaining organoids and add to the collection tube.

Check organoid suspension under microscope after 5 minutes and then as required to assess the dissociation of organoids. Use a P1000 to pipette the cell suspension up and down to help dissociate organoids. Stop incubation when organoids broken down to small clumps of cells. Avoid breaking organoids down to single cells Check if organoid suspension has broken down to small clumps of cells on a microscope.
Centrifuge at 800 x g, °C, for 2 minutes.

Aspirate supernatant to leave organoid cell pellet. If the organoid suspension does not pellet during centrifugation or there is a grey haze above the pellet (residual BME2), aspirate as much supernatant as possible. Re-suspend in ice cold PBS and repeat centrifugation. BME2 layer above prganoid pellet after centrifugation

Re-suspend pellet in appropriate amount of 80% BME2 ( 200 µL per well of a 6 well plate). Start by re-suspending pellet in a small volume to break down, then add additional BME2 to required volume. BME2 must be dispensed as quickly as possible as it will begin to set at room temperature. A cool block could be used to help keep the temperature down while plating. Avoid creating bubbles while re-suspending pellet.
Using a P200 pipette dispense a total of 200 µL organoid/BME2 suspension per well of a 6 well plate as 10 µL - 15 µL droplets.
Place in a 37 °C incubator (5% CO 2 ) for 15-30 minutes to allow BME2 to set.
Add 2 mL of appropriate organoid media per well of a 6 well plate.
Return to incubator. Media change twice a week until ready to passage.

Aspirate media from wells and add 1 mL or 2 mL TrypLE to each well. If any of the following are true collect organoids and media in a tube and centrifuge to pellet before adding TrypLE BME2 droplets detached from plate and floating in media Lots of cells or organoids floating in the media Lots of fibroblasts attached to the plate
